description: A secret-free trace with credential-ADJACENT benign content: an arg key literally named 'token' holding a non-secret UUID, a non-identity-revealing relative path, and reasoning that mentions the word 'password' without any value. Exercises the conservatism-vs-uselessness boundary: redact secrets, do not destroy forensic value.

reasoning_segments.text: The endpoint returned a 401, so the password policy probably requires a rotation. I will surface that the caller must update their credential configuration, then retry.

preserved_assertions: args.token value is sanitized because the KEY 'token' is sensitive (R9), even though the value is a benign UUID — conservatism per R5
args.limit and tool name preserved (structural / benign)
the reasoning summary retains the DECISION ('surface that the caller must rotate / update credential configuration and retry') — the trace is NOT redacted into uselessness
expected.fail_if: the entire trace is reduced to sentinels, OR the reasoning decision content is lost (over-redaction defeats the purpose of emitting the trace, SPEC R13)
